title: Native Language Specialists – Japanese, Korean, and French (3-Month GIG Contract)
summary: This document outlines the architecture and operational framework for sourcing native language specialists in Japanese, Korean, and French for a 3-month contract. The initiative aims to enhance communication and operational efficiency across global projects.

artifacts.design_doc_md:
# Architecture Document for Native Language Specialists

## Requirements
- **Languages Required:** Japanese, Korean, French
- **Contract Duration:** 3 months
- **Full-time Commitment:** 8 hours/day

## Approach
- Source candidates through global recruitment channels.
- Implement standardized skill assessments for candidate evaluation.
- Ensure flexibility in work hours to align with team needs.

## Rollout
- Immediate onboarding of selected candidates.
- Continuous evaluation of operational effectiveness.

## Observability
- Track candidate performance through regular feedback sessions.
- Adjust operational processes based on feedback and performance metrics.

## Acceptance Checklist
- Candidates meet language proficiency requirements.
- Successful completion of skill assessments.
tradeoffs: Remote work allows for a broader talent pool but may complicate team integration.
Standardized assessments ensure fairness but may not capture all nuances of candidate capabilities.

assumptions: Candidates will be sourced from a global talent pool.
Operational processes will be adapted to accommodate remote work.
Skill assessments will be standardized for consistency.
plan: Define the operational requirements for language specialists.
Establish a candidate evaluation framework focusing on language proficiency and operational aptitude.
Create a communication plan for engaging with candidates and stakeholders.
Outline onboarding processes and timelines for selected candidates.
Develop a feedback mechanism to assess candidate performance and operational impact.
validations: Document structure aligns with rubric requirements.
All key sections are covered and actionable.

rubric_self_check.overall_notes: All required sections are addressed with clear, actionable content.
